source: Adapted from uiux specifications/Kvdf UIUX System For Codex for Kabeeri's existing knowledge/design_system structure.
principle: Business Type -> Views -> Data -> Flows -> UI Contract -> Review Checklist, with creative variation driven by product answers.

patterns.name: Landing Page
patterns.primary_goal: Convert visitors around one clear offer.
ux_rules: Keep one dominant CTA per viewport.
Show proof before asking for commitment.
Use expressive motion only when it improves storytelling.

patterns.name: Corporate Website
patterns.primary_goal: Build trust, explain capabilities, and route visitors to contact or service pages.
ux_rules: Make credibility visible early.
Use structured service navigation.
Keep forms short and clear.
anti_patterns: Abstract visuals with no business signal.

patterns.name: E-commerce
patterns.primary_goal: Help users discover, compare, trust, and buy products.
ux_rules: Price, availability, shipping, and primary purchase action must be clear.
Disable impossible actions such as out-of-stock add to cart.
Use trust signals near purchase decisions.

patterns.name: Marketplace
patterns.primary_goal: Support discovery, comparison, trust, and multi-party transactions.
ux_rules: Expose seller trust clearly.
Keep filters fast and visible.
Separate platform, seller, and buyer responsibilities.

patterns.name: SaaS Product
patterns.primary_goal: Help users understand value, start quickly, and succeed inside the product.
ux_rules: First-run empty states must guide setup.
Settings must be grouped by mental model.
Use success feedback after configuration changes.
anti_patterns: No path from empty state to setup.

patterns.name: Admin Panel
patterns.primary_goal: Let operators manage data quickly and safely.
ux_rules: Tables need search, filter, pagination, empty, loading, and error states.
Destructive actions require confirmation.
Use density appropriate for repeat work.

patterns.name: Dashboard
patterns.primary_goal: Turn data into fast operational understanding.
ux_rules: Every chart needs context or a decision it supports.
Filters must be visible and persistent.
Show loading and no-data states for each data region.
anti_patterns: All cards with the same visual weight.

patterns.name: CRM
patterns.primary_goal: Help teams track relationships, pipeline, tasks, and activity.
ux_rules: Next action should be visible on customer and deal pages.
Pipeline movement needs clear feedback.
Activity history must be scanable.

patterns.name: ERP
patterns.primary_goal: Support complex operations with clarity, control, and traceability.
ux_rules: Use compact but readable density.
Keep role permissions and record status visible.
Prioritize accuracy over decorative styling.
anti_patterns: Large decorative cards for dense work.

patterns.name: Booking
patterns.primary_goal: Help users choose service, time, details, and confirmation without anxiety.
ux_rules: Available and unavailable slots must be unmistakable.
Show booking summary before confirmation.
Cancellation policy must be visible.

patterns.name: Delivery
patterns.primary_goal: Track order status across customer, store, driver, and admin views.
ux_rules: Current order status must always be clear.
Reduce uncertainty during waiting.
Live updates need stable fallback states.
anti_patterns: No support path for failed delivery.

patterns.name: LMS / EdTech
patterns.primary_goal: Help learners find courses, study, complete tasks, and track progress.
ux_rules: Progress must be visible.
Next lesson must be easy to reach.
Lesson screens should minimize distraction.

patterns.name: FinTech
patterns.primary_goal: Enable financial actions with trust, precision, and explicit confirmation.
ux_rules: Amounts and fees must be clear.
Every financial action needs review then confirm.
Success and failure states must be explicit.
anti_patterns: Animating amounts in a distracting way.

patterns.name: HealthTech
patterns.primary_goal: Present health information, appointments, records, and care actions clearly.
ux_rules: Readability and calm language matter more than decoration.
Critical information must be prominent.
Support older users with generous spacing and clear labels.

patterns.name: Real Estate
patterns.primary_goal: Help users search, compare, inspect, save, and contact about properties.
ux_rules: Photos, price, and location must be first-class.
Filters should support serious comparison.
Contact CTA must stay easy to find.
anti_patterns: Delayed image loading caused by motion.

patterns.name: AI Product
patterns.primary_goal: Help users provide input, understand processing, review output, and iterate.
ux_rules: Show what the user can ask or upload.
Processing must be visible but not theatrical.
Copy, regenerate, and feedback actions must be accessible.
anti_patterns: Fake thinking animations that run too long.
Generated content made harder to read.
